title: Business Analyst – Retail Buying & Merchandising Planning (12-Months FTC)
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way. **Technology at Primark** What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. Our technology team is actively shaping the next wave of advancements. Engaged with innovative initiatives, your expertise will propel our business into the future. Collaborating with a creative team of tech enthusiasts, you’ll contribute your unique skills to fuel our technological advancements. **What You’ll Do as Business Analyst – Planning and Ranging (12\-Months FTC)** In your role, you’ll collaborate with different people across a range of skillsets. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Serves as a core Technology role within the Analysis Practice, supporting the successful delivery of projects and programmes across the Technology portfolio and allocated based on skills, experience, and domain expertise. * Leads the identification, capture, prioritisation, and validation of business and technical requirements and user stories, working closely with Product Owners and business stakeholders to ensure solutions meet Primark’s strategic goals. * Analyses and documents as‑is and to‑be business processes, identifies improvement opportunities, and translates business needs into product‑based delivery requirements. * Owns end‑to‑end requirements traceability across the delivery lifecycle (design, build, test, and deployment), collaborating with development teams to ensure solutions align with business needs. * Supports quality and deployment activities, including QA and UAT planning, defect triage and prioritisation, and acting as a key contact during hypercare for user‑reported issues post‑deployment. * Works collaboratively across Product, Technology, business teams, partners, and suppliers, championing Primark’s business analysis methodology, contributing to the Community of Practice, and supporting discovery, business cases, training, and adoption of new systems and processes. **What You’ll Get** This role is a hybrid opportunity, offering 3 days Working from home. People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Minimum 3 years’ hands‑on experience in a Business Analysis or business relationship management role within a retail environment, ideally with Buying \& Merchandising expertise and experience delivering Assortment Planner or WSSI solutions. * Strong knowledge of the software delivery lifecycle, system design concepts, and system capabilities, with proven experience working as a Business Analyst in a software development context. * Practical experience operating within Agile delivery frameworks such as Scrum or Kanban, and using project and backlog management tools including Jira, Azure DevOps, and Confluence. * Demonstrated retail and/or ecommerce domain expertise, supported by a logical, analytical, and detail‑oriented mindset with strong problem‑solving and business interpretation skills. * Exceptional verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to translate complex technical concepts for non‑technical stakeholders and articulate business needs to technical teams. * Relevant degree, equivalent qualification, or experience, with desirable attributes including awareness of industry technologies and trends, familiarity with data tools (e.g. Power BI or Tableau), and professional certifications (IIBA, BCS, Scrum Alliance, or equivalent). Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. **REQ ID:** JR\-7231

title: Business Development / New Sales Associate
description: Wage: €32,000 \- €240,000\+ p/a **The Role:** Generating new business via new HVAC Design and Build, PPM, and Shiva Air Sterilisation Products sales. The ideal candidate will be used to a commission\-based sales position, with excellent communications skills, able to work on their own initiative, and be self\-driven. As there is no salary cap, it is up to the performance of the candidate to determine how high their salary can potentially be. There is easy potential to earn €200k\+ per annum in a commission for the right individual, with the right experience, contacts, and drive. Self\-employed persons, or Ltd companies / distribution agency’s willing to dedicate members of staff to this task may also suit this role. **The Products:** The products are company IP owned, patented, and self\-manufactured ‘Air Sterilisation’ Products at the premium end of the market. Training will be provided to the candidate to operate and to learn the benefits of these niche products. The target markets which have been successful to date include, but are not limited to: Medical \& Healthcare, Office Settings, Retail \& Hospitality, Government \& Local Authorities, Schools \& Colleges. Previous experience with government contracts and/or dealing with the HSE (Ireland) is an advantage and is encouraged to be declared on application. There is a minimum quarterly sales target to meet, to ensure neither the company, nor the candidate is wasting each other’s time. Warm sales leads which naturally generate weekly via branding and advertisement campaigns will be provided to the candidate, however the ideal candidate will be expected to have their own methods of generating leads via existing contacts. This position is focused on the island of Ireland, (north and south). However, if a candidate has potential access for export sales markets\- it is welcome, but should be declared in the application as we currently have arrangements for distributors in markets including the UK, EU, US, and UAE. Job Type: Full\-time, Permanent Salary: €32,000\.00\-€240,000\.00\+ per year Benefits: * Guaranteed base retainer amount circa €600\+/week * Fixed Commission percentage on new sales * Flexible schedule * Work from home * Company Vehicle * Laptop, Tablet, Phone * Company Pension Scheme Expected start date: 19/04/2023  #### **Address** Shiva Air Conditioning Unit 8, Ballymount Cross Business Park, Ballymount Road Lower, Ballymount, Dublin 24, D24 PYK2  #### **Phone** (01\) 4539118  #### **Email** info@shiva.ie

title: Business Development Executive
description: **Business Development Executive** **The Opportunity: Own the Whole Deal** * **Clear Pathway to Full Sales Cycle:** Move seamlessly from prospecting to closing. * **Client\-Facing from Day One:** Get direct exposure to decision\-makers immediately. * **End\-to\-End Ownership:** Take your own leads all the way from introductory pitch to final handshake. Ready to take the leap from appointment setting to closing? Ready to thrive in an environment where you don’t just pass off leads, but actually drive the deal from prospect to pitch to close? **Archer Recruitment** is expanding our Business Development team. We partner with top\-tier companies across Dublin and Ireland to deliver exceptional tech talent. As we expand our footprint beyond Ireland, we are growing our Business Development team to capitalise on a booming market. **In this role, you will:** * **Generate New Business:** Identify, pitch, and sign new clients to expand our market share. * **Expand Existing Accounts:** Nurture and build upon established client relationships to maximise revenue. * **Master the Sales Process:** Receive hands\-on training across the entire lifecycle—from advanced prospecting to high\-stakes closing and long\-term account management. **Why Join Archer?** * **Transparent Progression:** Total clarity on your career milestones and promotion pathways. * **Comprehensive Training:** Continuous career development and structured mentorship. * **True Ownership:** Control your own pipeline, your career trajectory, and your earning potential. **What You Bring to the Table:** * **Experience:** Ideally, 1\+ years in a sales role (prospecting, booking meetings, or outbound sales). * **Work Ethic:** A strong willingness to collaborate in our Dublin office, **Monday to Friday** . * **Eligibility:** A valid EU Passport or Stamp 4 Visa. **Compensation \& Rewards** * **Year 1 Earning Potential:** €50,000 average earnings (Base \+ Commission \+ Bonuses). * **Competitive Base:** Mid\-thirties starting range, tailored to your experience. * **Compounding Commission:** Paid monthly, featuring both **Upfront** and **Recurring** commission structures. As your book of business grows, so does your paycheck. * **Clear Earning Trajectory:** **Year 2:** €80,000\+, **Year 3:** €100,000\+ **Ready to Elevate Your Sales Career?** To apply or learn more, contact **Saoirse Lawton** today: **Email:** saoirse.lawton@archer.ie **, or call** \+353 (0\)1 649 8517

title: Business Development Representative
description: *If you are a current employee who is interested in applying to this position, please navigate to the internal Careers site to apply.* **About MarketStar** In everything we do, we believe in creating growth, for our clients, our employees, and our community. For the past 35\+ years, we have been generating revenue for the most innovative tech companies across the globe through our outsourced B2B demand, sales, customer success, and revenue operations solutions. We are passionate about cultivating career advancements for our people, and support them through mentorship, leadership, and career\-development programs. We provide service and support to our communities through the MarketStar Foundation. Our exceptional team is the cornerstone of MarketStar's accomplishments. We are proud of our award\-winning workplace culture and to be named a top employer in our industry. These achievements are a testament to our six core values, embraced by our 3,000\+ employees worldwide. From our headquarters in Utah, USA, to our global offices in India, Ireland, Bulgaria, Mexico, the Philippines, and Australia, we all work together to drive innovation and success. We are excited to have you apply to join our MarketStar team and can’t wait to discuss how we can help you find growth! **About The Business Development Representative** MarketStar is looking for a sales professional to represent our client as they continue their successful expansion into the enterprise corporate space. This is an exceptional opportunity to be a strategic leader on a pivotal team at the forefront of the latest technology and work with one of the top three companies in the world. Your responsibility will be to generate and qualify sales‑qualified opportunities within complex enterprise and corporate accounts. You will be a key contributor to MarketStar's mission by developing and executing strategic outreach plans and acting as a subject matter expert on how our client’s solutions can transform business operations. You will cover the EMEA Enterprise and Corporate markets, but may also provide mentorship and strategic support across other regions. **Location:** Hybrid, Leopardstown, Dublin 18 (Tuesday, Wednesday \& Thursday in office) **What Will You Do?** * Proactively seek new business opportunities in the market * Contact potential clients through cold calls and emails with the power of the brand behind you * Explore new business opportunities within certain target industries and personas * Present product value to potential clients * Identify client needs and suggest appropriate solutions * Proactively seek new business opportunities in the market * Set up meetings or calls between (prospective) clients and enterprise sales team * Adhere to daily, weekly and monthly KPIs * Achieve established sales goals and objectives * Serve as product and service advocate for the client, build interest and awareness of client’s value proposition, and assess mutual fit * Learn and maintain a current, comprehensive knowledge of client’s products and services * Champion our success stories and share with targeted companies in interesting industries and territories. **What Will You Need to Succeed?** * Fluent in English (Read, Write \& Speak) * 2 years of BDR experience * Experience working with IT decision makers * Experience with outreach sales strategies and prospecting companies in title: Business Development Representative - Italy

Understand the customers’ needs and challenges using sales methodologies, i.e. BANT * Carry out market research to broaden the customer base of HubSpot **What are the role requirements?** * The desire title: CLO Deal Administrator - 12 Month Fixed Term Contract
description: At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions, enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed in the right ways, all more confidently and more often—that’s what we call the courage to thrive. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever\-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One. As a wholly owned subsidiary of U.S. Bank, Elavon is committed to building the platforms and ecosystems that help over 1\.5 million customers around the world to achieve their financial goals—no matter what they need. From transaction processing to customer service, to driving innovation and launching new products, we’re building a range of tailored payment solutions powered by the latest technology. As part of our team, you can explore what motivates and energizes your career goals: partnering with our customers, our communities, and each other. We actively uphold transparent and fair hiring practices that support individual opportunity, inclusive culture, and career mobility across all levels of our organisation. We offer meaningful opportunities for growth, a culture of inclusion, and a strong commitment to transparency and integrity in everything we do. **Job Description** **CLO Deal Administrator \- 12 Month Fixed Term Contract** The CLO division sits within US Bank’s Global Corporate Trust (GCT) division. CLO’s are structured finance vehicles that issue both debt and equity liabilities, with the proceeds being used to buy a portfolio of collateral. Typical types of collateral include syndicated bank loans, corporate bonds \& equity. The CLO Deal Administrator role is a key role within our Corporate Trust Business and offers a good level of individual responsibility and accountability, whilst being part of a dynamic and successful business line with opportunity for future growth and development. Responsibilities of this role include: oversight and execution of bond/bank loan trade settlements, analysis of Money Market Fund investment requirements, booking cash inflows/outflows to the portfolio, tracking asset attributes such as accrual information, providing cash \& asset reporting to the clients on a daily basis, and assisting the Analysts with reporting to the CLO investors. The Deal Administrator ensures 100% accuracy and timeliness of data through the booking and reconciliation of account activity. Context for this role The role holder will be required to build and maintain effective relationships across the Bank and its parent, as well as with external parties, clients, and stakeholders. The role holder will be part of a specialist team and will be expected to exhibit teamwork, attention to detail, ability to work to tight deadlines, detailed technical knowledge with the ability to manage a heavy and varied workload. **Key Responsibilities** * Cash \& Asset Reconciliation responsibility for a portfolio of CDO/CLO transactions * Generate accurate Daily Cash Reports that reconcile asset payments and receivables on an Actual vs Expected model basis * Investigate and resolve any exceptions / discrepancies arising. Ability to resolve transactional and portfolio related issues * Process Broadly Syndicated Loan trades from trade ticket to settlement/payment request stage * Analysis and oversight of automated Trade settlement processes * Daily Review of available cash balances \& Money Market Funds to ensure sufficient liquidity to action trade settlements * Receive Bond/Securities tickets and Loan Trade Documents from Clients, review for accuracy, complete relevant documentation and liaise with internal Operations Team’s to ensure they settle within agreed time scales in the market * Liaise directly with Clients/Counterparties/Agent Banks and other departments for Bond/Loan \& Cash related queries * Settle Loan and Bond transactions on internal compliance system – CDO Suite * Reconcile Cash \& Asset Balances with our clients to support CDO Analytics in the production of Monthly CDO Investor Report **Experience Required** * Typically 1\-2 years experience within Financial Services with experience of finance / cash recs / settlements /or in a relatable Operations Analyst role. * Open to graduates with some internship / entry level experience related to the role / skills needed. * Preferably with any knowledge of fixed income, debt, loans or derivatives and a passion to enter the CLO market or some previous CLO experience or theoretical knowledge of CLO's be hugely beneficial * Looking for someone with a good level of numeracy and attention to detail * Excellent verbal and written communication skills and a client centric approach * Ability to work both independently and as a team player * Strong organizational skills and detail\-oriented * Relationship building across several internal groups \& divisions * Ability to manage a heavy and varied workload while demonstrating strong attention to detail * Indenture/Credit Agreement interpretation abilities and analytical skills are preferrable **Location expectations** This role requires working from a U.S. Bank location three (3\) or more days per week. This role is aligned to our Cherrywood Office **Accessibility** We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need adjustments at any stage of the application or hiring process, please contact your recruiter for guidance and support. **Total Rewards** U.S. Bank is committed to fair, equitable, and transparent compensation practices in line with local regulatory and legal requirements. Our total rewards approach is designed to attract, retain, and support top talent while ensuring equal pay for work of equal value. We offer a market\-competitive compensation package that includes: * Clearly defined salary ranges aligned with industry benchmarks and internal equity standards. * Performance\-based incentives for eligible employees (as defined by relevant plan rules), awarded through transparent, objective criteria that recognize both individual and company performance. * Inclusive equitable benefits that are accessible to all employees and focused around our 3 main pillars of financial wellbeing, health \& wellness). * Continuous development opportunities including training, education support, and career progression pathways based on inclusive and transparent criteria. * Employee recognition programs that celebrate achievements and milestones for all. We regularly review our compensation and benefits to ensure they remain competitive, inclusive, and responsive to employee needs and market trends. Further details of the compensation package will be provided upon application. We encourage candidates to explore the full value of our offer, including monetary and non\-monetary benefits, at Employee benefits and development \| U.S. Bank \| Elavon. **Closing Date** Posting may be closed earlier due to high volume of applicants. We aim to provide timely updates throughout the process and encourage early applications to ensure consideration.

Our Talent Acquisition Team welcomes all CV’s and will consider your application in line with the values, requirements and role competencies.**

title: Chef de Partie
description: Join the leadership team at the Arklow Bay Hotel, a well\-established 4\-star hotel set in the beautiful seaside town of Arklow, Co. Wicklow. With 91 bedrooms, many offering stunning bay views, and a reputation as one of Wicklow’s premier wedding, conference, and events destinations, we are now seeking an experienced and driven **Sous Chef** to support and lead our dynamic kitchen team. The hotel features extensive Conference \& Banqueting facilities for up to 450 guests, two popular dining outlets — The Pottery Restaurant and the Ferrybank Lounge — along with the Bay Health \& Leisure Club, which includes an 18\-metre pool, jacuzzi, steam room, sauna, gym, aerobics studio, and the Bay Beauty Rooms offering a wide range of treatments. Salary dependent on experience. **Join Our Kitchen Team** We are currently looking for a passionate and experienced Chef de Partie to join our dynamic kitchen team. This is an exciting opportunity for a motivated chef who takes pride in delivering high\-quality dishes, thrives in a fast\-paced environment, and is eager to grow within a professional kitchen. If you are passionate about fresh ingredients, teamwork, and creating memorable dining experiences, we would love to hear from you. **The ideal candidate should have the following attributes:** * Previous experience in a Chef de Partie role of at least 12 months. * Team player with excellent communication skills. * Be able to assist Chef's in all areas of food preparation and service. * Have a good working knowledge of HACCP. * Comply with H\&S requirements. * Applicants need to be fluent in English. **Key Responsibilities** * Prepare, cook, and present dishes to a consistently high standard * Manage your own kitchen section during service * Ensure food hygiene and HACCP standards are maintained at all times * Assist with stock control, ordering, and minimising waste * Work closely with the kitchen team to ensure smooth service * Support junior chefs and help maintain a positive team culture * Ensure all food is prepared and served according to company standards * Maintain cleanliness and organisation throughout the kitchen **What We’re Looking For** * Previous experience as a Chef de Partie or strong experience as a Commis Chef ready to progress * Passion for food and attention to detail * Ability to work efficiently under pressure * Strong organisational and communication skills * Excellent knowledge of food safety and hygiene practices * A positive attitude and strong work ethic * Flexibility to work evenings, weekends, and holidays as required **What We Offer** * Competitive salary based on experience * Career progression opportunities * Staff meals provided during shifts * Supportive and professional working environment * Ongoing training and development * Flexible scheduling where possible * Employee discounts and benefits Salary depending on experience. If you are a motivated culinary professional ready to take the next step in your career within a busy and rewarding hotel environment, we would love to hear from you. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €35,000\.00\-€40,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Company events * Employee discount * Gym membership * On\-site gym * On\-site parking Language: * English (required) Work Location: In person

title: Chef de Partie
description: At Dean Group, we reimagine hospitality with character, not conformity. Founded in Dublin and now growing across Europe and the US, we create hotels shaped by personality, culture, and a heart\-led hustle. Each of our hotels, restaurants, bars and gyms tells its own story, brought to life through humble hospitality and genuine human connection. Our people are at the centre of it all. They show up with individuality, warmth and curiosity, shaping who we are and how guests experience our hotels every day. We welcome fresh thinking and imagination, because playing it safe never made anything unforgettable. We are currently seeking an experienced **Chef de Partie** to join the team in **The Devlin.** If you are passionate about food and have previous experience in a busy hotel then we would love to hear from you! **Key Areas** * Section \& Service Management * Team Collaboration \& Supervision * Quality Control \& Standards * Stock \& Operational Support **Job Purpose** The Chef de Partie is a vital member of the kitchen team, responsible for managing their own section efficiently and delivering exceptional food consistently. This role involves preparing, cooking, and presenting dishes with precision and care, supporting the Head and Sous Chefs, and helping to train junior staff. A strong eye for detail, excellent time management, and a passion for food are essential in this fast\-paced, quality\-driven environment. **Key Deliverables** **Section \& Service Management** * Prepare and cook dishes in your assigned section to the highest standard during service * Control and coordinate mise\-en\-place for your section, adapting to booking levels and menu changes * Maintain portion control, quality, and consistency of all dishes * Take ownership of the cleanliness and organisation of your station **Team Collaboration \& Supervision** * Supervise and support junior chefs in your section * Assist with training and mentoring of Commis Chefs and Kitchen Porters * Maintain clear communication with the Head Chef, Sous Chef, and other sections * Support a positive, respectful, and inclusive team culture **Quality Control \& Standards** * Monitor food quality and presentation throughout service * Follow all hygiene, safety, and food handling protocols * Ensure all food storage and labelling is in accordance with HACCP and kitchen SOPs * Assist in regular cleaning, deep cleaning, and maintenance tasks as needed **Stock \& Operational Support** * Track and manage stock levels for your section * Assist in ordering, checking, and rotating deliveries * Reduce waste and ensure efficient use of ingredients * Participate in regular stock takes and inventory management tasks **Preferred Experience** * Minimum 2 years' experience at Chef de Partie level * Strong culinary skills with a desire to progress and learn * High level of initiative and ability to work independently * Trustworthy, calm under pressure, and consistent in delivery * Excellent time management, organisation, and multitasking skills * Team\-focused with strong communication and leadership qualities * Full understanding of HACCP and food safety regulations * Flexibility to work varied shifts, including weekends and evenings **Benefits:** * Global discounted staff room rates across the group * 50% off Food and 25% Beverage across the group * Accrued Annual Leave with Service * Friends and Family discount * Gym Membership Discounts\* * Continual training and development * Career progression within the Group * Gratuities \* * Meals on shift * Bike to work scheme * Taxsaver for travel * Refer a friend scheme * Wellness at work events and access to wellness platform * Employment Assistance Programme * Group discounts for cinema tickets, shopping deals etc * Team appreciation days * Service recognition * Regular fun\-filled activities and gatherings for all teams across the group * Applicable to certain positions Dean Group owns and operates a number of hotel across Ireland, in certain instances we may feel your skills and experience may suit another role we have available. By submitting your application, you are permitting Dean Group to contact you for other opportunities within the Group \#LP

title: Chief Operating Officer
description: **About the Company and Position** Strata Financial is a fast\-growing full\-service accountancy firm strategically set to become one of Ireland’s Top 10 accounting firms by 2028, located across 6 offices in the North East/South East Corridor of the island of Ireland. As the group scales through both organic growth, and integration of other firms, we are now seeking to recruit a hands\-on Chief Operating Officer to build the operational engine that allows every office, team and newly acquired firm to perform consistently. This is a role for a practical operator — someone who can standardise, integrate, improve and execute. You will own the day\-to\-day operational agenda, create the systems and controls that support profitable growth, and turn each joining firm into a smoothly integrated part of the wider group. **Key Responsibilities** * Build and embed a consistent group\-wide operating model across compliance, audit, accounting, tax, advisory, bookkeeping, payroll and client onboarding. * Create clear workflows, file structures, review points, SLAs, quality controls, KPIs, dashboards and management rhythms across every office and service line. * Improve utilisation, recovery, WIP, lock\-up, write\-offs, client throughput and overall operational performance through stronger accountability and better data. * Remove duplication, manual processes and operational drag across legacy and acquired offices, replacing them with smarter processes, shared services and technology. * Own and rationalise the practice technology stack across practice management, document management, workflow, time and billing, payroll, ID and AML, and client portals. * Lead post\-acquisition integration through a repeatable 30/60/90/180\-day playbook covering systems, people, clients, processes, premises and brand. * Manage systems migrations end\-to\-end, including scoping, suppliers, data migration, parallel running, cutover, adoption and legacy system retirement, with no service disruption. * Work with HR, IT, Finance, Marketing, technical and risk teams to align contracts, roles, benefits, performance frameworks, ways of working, cultural issues and regulatory standards across ICAEW, ACCA, AAT and wider requirements. * Track integration milestones, cost\-to\-serve gains, system savings and synergies, reporting clearly to the CEO, CFO and board. * Support M\&A diligence by assessing systems debt, process maturity, staffing dependencies, client concentration, operational risk and realistic integration cost. * Chair integration governance and advise the leadership team on capacity, pricing, service\-line growth and capital deployment. **Experience/Requirements** * Proven experience as a COO, Operations Director, Head of Integration, Head of Transformation or similar within accountancy, professional services, legal, consulting, wealth management or another fee\-earning environment. * Direct buy\-and\-build or roll\-up experience, ideally PE\-backed, with hands\-on involvement in at least three completed integrations. * Strong knowledge of accountancy practice operations, including compliance workflow, advisory delivery, WIP, lock\-up, recovery, utilisation and partner economics. * Strong fluency with systems such as Drive, CCH, IRIS, Karbon, Xero, Sage, Dext, BrightPay, Senta, Inflo, FYI, Virtual Cabinet or equivalent platforms. * A track record of delivering systems migrations, operational KPIs, dashboards and governance in a partnership or partner\-led environment. * Relevant third level qualification would be useful, but credibility with partners and acquired\-firm principals matters more. * Commercially sharp, execution\-first, data\-led, politically astute, resilient, pragmatic and highly organised under pressure. This is not a strategy\-only seat, a deputy CEO role or a polished operating environment. Strata Financial is mid\-build, with working, but developing systems, developing processes and a lot more deals ahead. The mission is simple: fix it, scale it, and make every future integration easier and better than the last, and deliver effective excellence. **Remuneration Package** Highly Competitive Salary and Benefits. **Applications** Applications should include a CV and a short covering note setting out: (i) two integrations the candidate has personally led, (ii) the operational KPI they are most proud of having moved, and (iii) their view on the most common reason accountancy firm integrations underperform their deal model. **Contact** To apply, please submit your application or make an initial enquiry please contact: Yvonne McNulty, Senior Partner, at Brightwater Executive to stratafinancial@brightwaterexecutive.com

title: Civil Engineer
description: **Civil Engineer** ================== **Role Overview** We are seeking a Civil Engineer with 5\+ years’ experience to join our growing civil engineering team based in Dublin City Centre. The successful candidate will lead the design and delivery of site infrastructure to support development, including water, drainage and road design. The ideal applicant for this role will be eager to develop their skills and progress to a Senior role within the organisation. **About Us** CS Consulting is a leading civil, structural traffic engineering consultancy delivering practical, buildable solutions across Ireland and internationally. We work on technically diverse building and infrastructure projects, from concept through to delivery, with a strong focus on constructability, quality, and collaboration. We’re a hands\-on practice where engineers are trusted to lead, influence design, and shape project outcomes. We offer a supportive, solutions\-driven environment, meaningful project exposure, and the opportunity to play a key role in the continued growth and direction of the business. **Upcoming Projects** Our upcoming projects include all site development and infrastructure work (roads, traffic transport, drainage, water, utilities, bridges, etc.) from feasibility through planning, detailed design and construction for projects including: * Large\-scale residential projects for the Land Development Agency (LDA) and their Development Partners * Private residential projects (high\-rise and low\-rise) nationwide * Refurbishment of historic structures for the OPW and public/ private sector Clients * New\-build commercial office and hotel projects * Commercial office and hotel refurbishment and fit\-out projects * Industrial and data centre projects **Key Responsibilities** * Design of SuDs features * Design of Foul and Watermain Systems to Uisce Eireann Code of Practice and Local Authority requirements * Design of roads to comply with TII legislation DMURS * Prepare feasibility and planning stage reports including engineering services reports, flood risk assessments, etc. Develop responses to planning conditions to secure compliance. * Prepare tender and construction level designs and specifications. * Attend design team/ client meetings, participate in stakeholder engagement, and liaise with Local Authorities/ Statutory Bodies on behalf of clients. * Collaborate with civil and structural teams on integrated multidisciplinary projects. **Qualifications Experience** * Honours Degree (Level 8\+) in Civil Engineering. * Chartered Engineer (CEng MIEI) or ambition to achieve chartership in the near future. * Minimum 5 years’ relevant experience, with at least 2 years in an Irish consultancy. * Strong knowledge of water, drainage and road design standards from planning through construction. * Proficient in AutoCAD, road design software (Civil 3D desirable) and drainage modelling software (InfoDrainage or similar). * Full driving licence **Key Attributes** * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Capable of developing strong client relationships and representing the company at design team, local authority and public meetings. * Detail\-oriented with a strong technical foundation and practical site awareness. * Ambitious and proactive, with a commitment to CPD and technical leadership. * Comfortable working both independently and as part of a multidisciplinary team. **Why Join Us?** * Competitive Salary and Opportunities for Progression: We offer a competitive salary and bonus, as well as a clear pathway to Senior * Mid\-Size Firm with Large\-Scale Projects: Opportunity to take ownership of your work with the support of highly experienced Senior Engineers and Directors as well as a talented team of engineering and technician resources. * Broad Range of Projects with the Best Clients: We work across commercial, residential, healthcare, education, hotel, leisure and retail with Ireland's top public and private Clients including the Land Development Agency (LDA), Ronan Group Real Estate, McGarrell Reilly, Quintain, Westridge RE, DRES, Hibernia Real Estate Group, Bartra, Tetrarch, Local Authorities, University of Limerick, Ireland's Top Contractors, and many more. * Shape the way we work: Our Organisation builds on energetic colleagues with a desire to learn, contribute their experience and improve our way of working. We believe in innovation and improvement and utilise data analysis to learn and grow. * CPD Learning: Engineers Ireland approved CPD programme and regular lessons learned and knowledge sharing sessions in a round\-table format. * Leading Software Tech: Access to leading technical software and review our requirements regularly to ensure we are using the best and latest in technology.

We work on technically diverse building and infrastructure projects, from concept through to delivery, with a strong focus on constructability, quality, and collaboration. We’re a hands\-on practice where engineers are trusted to lead, influence design, and shape project outcomes. We offer a supportive, solutions\-driven environment, meaningful project exposure, and the opportunity to play a key role in the continued growth and direction of the business. **Upcoming Projects** Our Upcoming Projects Include All Site Development And Infrastructure Work (roads, Traffic Transport, Drainage, Water, Utilities, Bridges, Etc.) From Feasibility Through Planning, Detailed Design And Construction For Projects Including * Large\-scale residential projects for the Land Development Agency (LDA) and their Development Partners * Private residential projects (high\-rise and low\-rise) nationwide * Refurbishment of historic structures for the OPW and public/ private sector Clients * New\-build commercial office and hotel projects * Commercial office and hotel refurbishment and fit\-out projects * Industrial and data centre projects **Key Responsibilities** * Design of SuDs features * Design of Foul and Watermain Systems to Uisce Eireann Code of Practice and Local Authority requirements * Design of roads to comply with TII legislation DMURS * Prepare feasibility and planning stage reports including engineering services reports, flood risk assessments, etc. Develop responses to planning conditions to secure compliance. * Prepare tender and construction level designs and specifications. * Attend design team/ client meetings, participate in stakeholder engagement, and liaise with Local Authorities/ Statutory Bodies on behalf of clients. * Collaborate with civil and structural teams on integrated multidisciplinary projects. **Qualifications Experience** * Honours Degree (Level 8\+) in Civil Engineering. * Chartered Engineer (CEng MIEI) or ambition to achieve chartership in the near future. * Minimum 5 years’ relevant experience, with at least 2 years in an Irish consultancy. * Strong knowledge of water, drainage and road design standards from planning through construction. * Proficient in AutoCAD, road design software (Civil 3D desirable) and drainage modelling software (InfoDrainage or similar). * Full driving licence **Key Attributes** * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Capable of developing strong client relationships and representing the company at design team, local authority and public meetings. * Detail\-oriented with a strong technical foundation and practical site awareness. * Ambitious and proactive, with a commitment to CPD and technical leadership. * Comfortable working both independently and as part of a multidisciplinary team. **Why Join Us?** * Competitive Salary and Opportunities for Progression: We offer a competitive salary and bonus, as well as a clear pathway to Senior * Mid\-Size Firm with Large\-Scale Projects: Opportunity to take ownership of your work with the support of highly experienced Senior Engineers and Directors as well as a talented team of engineering and technician resources. * Broad Range of Projects with the Best Clients: We work across commercial, residential, healthcare, education, hotel, leisure and retail with Ireland's top public and private Clients including the Land Development Agency (LDA), Ronan Group Real Estate, McGarrell Reilly, Quintain, Westridge RE, DRES, Hibernia Real Estate Group, Bartra, Tetrarch, Local Authorities, University of Limerick, Ireland's Top Contractors, and many more. * Shape the way we work: Our Organisation builds on energetic colleagues with a desire to learn, contribute their experience and improve our way of working. We believe in innovation and improvement and utilise data analysis to learn and grow. * CPD Learning: Engineers Ireland approved CPD programme and regular lessons learned and knowledge sharing sessions in a round\-table format. * Leading Software Tech: Access to leading technical software and review our requirements regularly to ensure we are using the best and latest in technology.

title: Cloud Deployment Engineer (AI Security)
description: At F5, we strive to bring a better digital world to life. Our teams empower organizations across the globe to create, secure, and run applications that enhance how we experience our evolving digital world. We are passionate about cybersecurity, from protecting consumers from fraud to enabling companies to focus on innovation. Everything we do centers around people. That means we obsess over how to make the lives of our customers, and their customers, better. And it means we prioritize a diverse F5 community where each individual can thrive. We are looking for a Cloud Deployment Engineer (AI Security) to join our growing Professional Services AI Guardrails team. This is a customer\-facing role focused on delivering impactful cloud deployment solutions across public cloud environments such as AWS and Azure. The position provides a unique opportunity to design, implement, and optimize cloud\-based projects for our clients while acting as a trusted advisor for post\-deployment improvements, scaling and optimization. The role moves beyond traditional technical support by emphasizing proactive customer engagement, customized deployments, and infrastructure fine\-tuning, paving the way for career growth into Professional Services Consulting F5's broader Services portfolio. Success in this position requires strong technical expertise in cloud environments, strong project leadership and the ability to operate with high autonomy in ambiguous, fast\-paced environments. **Key Responsibilities:** **Cloud Deployment \& Advisory** * Own end\-to\-end deployment activities, from planning and configuration to implementation, ensuring project\-based delivery across AWS and Azure environments. * Develop and maintain comprehensive deployment playbooks, serving as a blueprint for consistent and efficient implementations. * Provide expert advisory services to clients, including best practices for infrastructure optimization, scaling, and fine\-tuning post\-deployment. * Advise customers on the deployment of AI Security and security orchestration, ensuring low\-latency performance for real\-time AI applications. * Collaborate closely with the Solution Engineering team to ensure a seamless transition from Proof of Concept (POC) to full\-scale production deployment. * Partner with customers to understand their unique requirements and ensure tailored solutions meet business objectives. **Customer Collaboration \& Ownership** * Act as a key point of contact for customers during deployment projects, ensuring high levels of communication, satisfaction, and project alignment. * Address customer feedback proactively by recommending and delivering enhancements to project implementations post\-deployment. * Conduct post\-deployment 'Health Checks' to analyze GPU utilization, recommending instance rightsizing (e.g., A100 vs. H100\) to balance inference speed with TCO (Total Cost of Ownership) * Build trust and transparency with customers by offering actionable guidance that strengthens long\-term relationships. **Technical Expertise** * Apply advanced technical knowledge of cloud platforms to design and implement cutting\-edge solutions tailored to client environments. * Maintain a deep understanding of F5’s products and services, harnessing their capabilities to solve customer business challenges. * Collaborate with engineering and product development teams when necessary to ensure smooth integration and support continuity. **Process Optimization \& Leadership** * Continuously evaluate deployment processes to improve efficiency, identify gaps, and enhance operational performance for our AI Security products. * Mentor and guide junior team members, sharing expertise to elevate overall team capabilities. * Take initiative to drive innovation within deployment projects, identifying opportunities for automation or enhanced orchestration. **Qualifications:** **Required Skills \& Experience:** **Technical Expertise:** * Practical experience (3\+ years) working with public cloud platforms (AWS, Azure), including cloud infrastructure setup, deployment, and optimization. * Experience with LLM deployment patterns (RAG, fine\-tuning) and an understanding of the security risks unique to generative AI. * Experience with GPU/CPU sizing tradeoffs for performance and cost balance * Proficiency in creating and managing technical documentation such as deployment playbooks or operational guides. * Fluency in scripting tools and languages (e.g., Python, Bash) to automate deployment processes. **Customer\-Facing Experience:** * Skilled in managing customer relationships with strong interpersonal and communication skills. * Ability to engage stakeholders and deliver tailored deployment solutions based on client needs. **Project Management:** * Foundational experience in managing deployment projects, including scoping, resource allocation, and delivering on defined timelines. * Strong problem\-solving skills with the ability to make judgment calls in fast\-paced and dynamic environments. **Cloud Technologies \& Tools:** * Hands\-on experience with monitoring and orchestration tools like Kubernetes, Terraform, Prometheus or similar systems. * Understanding of core cloud and web technologies (e.g., APIs, HTTP, DNS). * Solid knowledge of Linux environments and networking fundamentals. **Preferred Skills:** * Familiarity with F5 products is a plus but not mandatory. * Red Hat Certified Specialist in OpenShift Administration * Experience with post\-deployment infrastructure fine\-tuning or optimization techniques. The Job Description is intended to be a general representation of the responsibilities and requirements of the job. However, the description may not be all\-inclusive, and responsibilities and requirements are subject to change. **Please note that F5 only contacts candidates through F5 email address (ending with @f5\.com) or auto email notification from Workday (ending with f5\.com or** **@myworkday.com)** **.** **Equal Employment Opportunity** It is the policy of F5 to provide equal employment opportunities to all employees and employment applicants without regard to unlawful considerations of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, sensory, physical, or mental disability, marital status, veteran or military status, genetic information, or any other classification protected by applicable local, state, or federal laws. This policy applies to all aspects of employment, including, but not limited to, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, discipline, and termination. F5 offers a variety of reasonable accommodations for candidates. Requesting an accommodation is completely voluntary. F5 will assess the need for accommodations in the application process separately from those that may be needed to perform the job. Request by contacting accommodations@f5\.com.

title: Cloud Support Engineer

tooling to support efficient software delivery * Monitor system performance, availability and capacity, proactively addressing issues and improving resilience * Manage system upgrades, patching title: Commercial Analyst
description: **Tech that takes you places** We’re CarTrawler, the global travel tech company behind seamless connections between people, places and possibilities. From powering car rental to creating smarter ways to move, we make travel smoother for millions worldwide. Our culture is built on curiosity, collaboration and craic where every idea counts and every journey matters. Ready to make an impact? Let’s go places together. The Commercial Analyst plays a critical role in supporting the Head of P\&L, Commercial Manager and Trading Squad in making informed business decisions. Working within a cross\-functional, self\-organised Trading Squad, the Commercial Analyst will drive insights, analyse performance, and communicate key trends to stakeholders. The role requires strong commercial acumen and the ability to identify, prioritise, and act upon opportunities for growth, focusing on driving profitable outcomes across multiple partners and markets. The Commercial Analyst will also collaborate across the wider Trading Analyst Group, ensuring that learnings are captured, shared, and implemented across the organisation. Additionally, the role will involve project work and providing analytical support for various initiatives, ensuring that data\-driven strategies are consistently aligned with the organisation's broader goals. **Your Journey starts here!** **What you’ll do as a Commercial Analyst** **Performance Analysis \& Communication:** * Analyse trading performance and identify the key drivers influencing outcomes. * Communicate findings to stakeholders in a clear, tailored way to ensure insights are easily understood and actionable. **Project Leadership \& Co\-management:** * Support ad\-hoc and long\-term projects, such as partner performance optimisation and strategic initiatives, by providing data insights and recommendations. **P\&L Deep Dives:** * Conduct regular financial P\&L deep dives on selected partner groups, providing actionable insights for performance improvement. **Reporting Framework Development:** * Develop scalable, automated reporting that allow channel and P\&L owners to analyse performance and identify trends effectively. **P\&L Performance Communication:** * Ensure that performance trends related to pricing, digital merchandising, content, and conversion are well communicated, contextualised, and prioritised to drive business growth. **Collaboration and Action:** * Work closely with the Trading Squad to identify growth opportunities and ensure these are developed and executed across the business. * Collaborate with other teams (e.g., business, product, finance) to ensure commercial strategies are aligned and backed by robust analytical insights. **Project Support:** * Provide analytical support for cross\-functional projects, offering insights from performance data to shape strategy and ensure alignment with business goals. * Contribute to special initiatives, such as pricing forums, new product launches, or partner onboarding, by supporting the data analysis and performance tracking. **What you’ll bring to the team** * At least 3\+ years in a similar role, with a proven track record in data analysis, identifying commercial opportunities, and driving business results. * A third\-level degree in IT, Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, or a related field. * Strong experience working with large datasets and practical experience with SQL. * Expertise in data visualisation, including creating interactive dashboards with tools such as Tableau or ThoughtSpot. * A passion for data and the ability to use it to identify commercial opportunities and product improvements. * A strong understanding of Excel and PowerPoint are essential. * Ability to clearly and succinctly communicate complex findings to a non\-specialist audience. * Strong collaboration and communication skills, working effectively across teams and with external partners. **Nice to Have:** * Statistical Programming: * Experience with statistical programming languages like R or Python is a plus, though not essential. **Go on, give it a go!** We know that looking for a new job can feel a bit daunting but don’t let a touch of imposter syndrome hold you back. Even if you don’t tick every box, we’d still love to hear from you! Apply today to stay connected. At CarTrawler, we’re proud to be an equal opportunities employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for everyone. No matter your background, identity, or life experience, if you’re passionate about what you do, you’ll be welcomed here. **Perks for the journey** · Competitive pay and benefits, including a performance\-based annual bonus, fully subsidised VHI health insurance and pension matching up to 4%. · Generous time off and flexible working, including 25 days’ annual leave, extra company days, monthly Friday Unplugged afternoons and the option to work abroad for up to 20 days a year. · Comprehensive wellbeing support, including access to health and mental health programmes (HeadsUp and EAP) and paid leave for marriage, volunteering and personal wellbeing including menstrual, menopause, and fertility leave. · Growth and recognition culture with development opportunities through training, coaching and study support plus programmes that celebrate individual and team achievements. · Everyday perks that make a difference \- subsidised canteen (KC Peaches), on\-site car parking, Bike to Work and TaxSaver schemes plus an active Sports \& Social Club.

title: Compliance Analyst

reviews, supporting investigations, and enhancing compliance policies within a supportive team environment. **What you'll do:** * Conduct thorough reviews of email communications for trading groups, Assist in the development and enhancement of compliance policies and procedures, adapting them to reflect changes in business operations, regulations, and emerging issues. * Stay up\-to\-date with Securities Act 1933, Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (FSMA). * Excellent team player who thrives on co\-operation and collaboration within diverse groups. * Ability to prioritise tasks applications from all candidates**

title: Compliance Officer, Consumer Compliance, Associate, Dublin
description: **Job Description** **GLOBAL COMPLIANCE AT GOLDMAN SACHS** At Goldman Sachs, the mandate of Global Compliance is to prevent, detect and mitigate compliance, regulatory, and reputational risk across the firm and help to strengthen the firm’s culture of compliance. Compliance accomplishes this mandate through the firm’s enterprise\-wide compliance risk management program. As an independent control function and part of the firm’s second line of defence, Compliance assesses the firm’s compliance, regulatory and reputational risk; monitors for compliance with new or amended laws, rules and regulations; designs and implements controls, policies, procedures and training; conducts independent testing; investigates, surveils and monitors for compliance risks and breaches; and leads the firm’s responses to regulatory examinations, audits and inquiries. Compliance provides advice on how to conduct the firm’s business in a manner that complies with the vast array of rules, regulations and regulatory expectations around the globe. Importantly, Compliance plays a key role in reviewing and effectively challenging transactions presenting compliance or reputational risks. **Business Unit And Role Overview** As part of the Consumer Compliance team, you will provide compliance support and coverage to the Deposits business. You will also participate in region\-wide and global initiatives to enact the Global Compliance mandate and Business Unit priorities. **Role And Responsibilities** To fulfil this role you will need to: * Have consumer regulatory experience on a variety of issues relating to the support of a consumer\-focused business, including new business initiatives, customer servicing and communications, and changes to existing product and service offerings * Support the development and oversight of a jurisdictional\-specific compliance risk management program within a global financial institution, utilising existing enterprise risk management frameworks * Have a strong background / familiarity with consumer protection rules from an EU jurisdiction * Support the development, ongoing management and oversight of a full suite of compliance policies to ensure both the deposits business and its employees operate in a compliant manner * Advise on and approve consumer marketing and advertising materials relating to consumer accounts * Ensure adherence to EU regulations, including implementation of new regulations and regulatory change management * Liaise with other Compliance Officers in the Goldman Sachs Compliance Division globally as well as other stakeholders throughout the firm to ensure adherence to firmwide compliance standards **Qualifications And Skills** A strong candidate for this role will satisfy the following criteria: * At least 3\-5\+ years of work experience as a Compliance Officer/Professional preferably focused on EU consumer protection regulations and Irish consumer protection regulation * Strong understanding of Marketing and Advertising regulations as they apply to consumer products. * Understanding of the different regulatory requirements during the lifecycle of consumer products (e.g. product and service design through to implementation, ongoing customer support and marketing) * Familiarity with digital customer journeys in financial services * Bachelor’s degree or higher * Experience in interacting with regulators, or supporting regulatory audits or inquiries * Familiarity with legal entity governance and committee management * Clear, concise communications skills; able to communicate with impact at senior levels * Able and willing to challenge; comfortable with inter\-personal conflict; able to build strong professional rapport with stakeholders while maintaining independence as a compliance professional * Work independently. Exercise sound judgment, ethics and integrity * Fast learner; able to adapt to business and regulatory changes; able to challenge status quo and find new solutions * Well organised with the ability to manage both longer term projects and daily, often time\-sensitive, escalations * Strong analytical, technical and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to exercise sound and balanced judgement * Take ownership of tasks, progress them independently, and escalate to senior stakeholders when required * Work collaboratively and inclusively to come up with solutions to identify and mitigate compliance risks **About Goldman Sachs** At Goldman Sachs, we commit our people, capital and ideas to help our clients, shareholders and the communities we serve to grow. Founded in 1869, we are a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm. Headquartered in New York, we maintain offices around the world. We believe who you are makes you better at what you do. We're committed to fostering and advancing diversity and inclusion in our own workplace and beyond by ensuring every individual within our firm has a number of opportunities to grow professionally and personally, from our training and development opportunities and firmwide networks to benefits, wellness and personal finance offerings and mindfulness programs. Learn more about our culture, benefits, and people at GS.com/careers. We’re committed to finding reasonable accommodations for candidates with special needs or disabilities during our recruiting process. Goldman Sachs is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, colour, religion, sex, national origin, age, veterans status, disability, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

title: Construction Project Planner

XYZ has grown to over 100 staff across the UK, US \& Europe and is working with Mission Critical organisations \& construction companies to successfully deliver major projects. **Project Planner Opportunity** Our model combines a new age of 4D Scheduling with objective, real\-time insights using Augmented Reality for quality and progress tracking. Programme(s) in accordance with Client requirements into the 3D design * Expanding the scheduled activities including MEP programme (Mechanical, Electrical and Plumbing) to sequence the build and integrate within the BIM model * Working with the Sales Team on client calls and demonstrating good schedule and construction knowledge * Project Data analytics with Microsoft Power BI, Power Apps and Excel * Regular progress reporting at both a customer and company level * Monitoring productivity and completion deadlines * This is a front\-end role, so you’ll be in regular interaction with stakeholders * Responsible for technical and assurance support for Planning \& Project Controls in the XYZ Project Team. **Essential Experience Required** * 2\+ years Scheduling experience * Mission content for international audiences and markets · Support localisation and content quality initiatives across global markets, including Chinese\-language content where required · Localise and title: Corporate Finance Associate
description: Barden have been exclusively engaged by a well known Irish company that is significantly growing and set to double in size within the next year. They are now inviting three corporate finance professional to join their team as **Corporate Finance Associate** . This is a unique opportunity at this level sitting on a close knit team, gaining direct exposure to a broad range of activities working closely with the senior leadership team. **ABOUT THE ROLE:** This role provides an excellent opportunity to utilise one’s corporate finance experience within an industry setting and add real value to a leading Irish business. Main duties and responsibilities will include: * Support the execution of acquisitions, disposals, and financing activities. * Assist with the development of financial models, investment analysis, and performance forecasting. * Contribute to due diligence efforts and manage data flow and documentation for live transactions. * Help create high\-impact presentations, internal papers, and investor materials. * Collaborate with internal stakeholders across multiple departments to gather insights and input for transaction work. * Liaise with external partners, including advisors, lenders, and investors, throughout deal lifecycles. * Monitor financial obligations and covenant compliance tied to active financing structures. * Identify areas for process improvement across processes. * Ad hoc projects as required. **ABOUT THE PERSON:** * ACA or CFA Qualified with 0\-3 years PQE; * Previous corporate finance, transactions services or investment experience a strong advantage; * Strong financial modelling skills essential; * Advanced Excel skills; * Ability to deliver both qualitative and quantitative analysis for decision making at senior management level; * Good commercial acumen and ability to analyse qualitative information; * Good problem solving and analytical skills; * Excellent working knowledge of Excel. ***This is an ideal role for someone looking for the opportunity to advance their career in Corporate Finance and progress within a progressive and significantly growing company.*** *Barden’s Accounting and Tax recruitment practice is where finance professionals go before they start looking for a job. Make sure you get expert advice about your Accounting / Tax career from Barden first – your future is just too important to leave to chance.*

title: Customer Growth Associate, Google Customer Solutions, Dutch Market (English, Dutch)
description: ### **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience. * Experience in digital sales, client management, customer service, business development, customer outreach, marketing, consulting, digital advertising, or related field. * Ability to communicate in English and Dutch fluently to engage with clients in the region. ### **Preferred qualifications:** * Familiarity with Google Ads and online advertising, with Google Digital Workshop certification or equivalent experience. * Ability to build compelling narratives and utilize storytelling as a client engagement strategy. * Ability to influence stakeholders internally and externally to deliver impact. * Ability to own an advertising or media sales portfolio in the Netherlands, and consistently overachieve quotas to drive growth. **About the job** ----------------- Businesses of all shapes and sizes rely on Google’s unparalleled advertising solutions to help them grow in today's dynamic marketing environment. You will act like an owner, move with velocity through change, finding innovative ways to consistently deliver extraordinary and incremental outcomes for both Google and your customers. As a member of our team, you’ll have the opportunity to work with company owners and make a real difference in their businesses by helping them grow. Together, we help shape the future of innovation for customers, partners, and sellers...and we have fun doing it.**Responsibilities** -------------------- * Deliver against ambitious quarterly business growth and product growth goals. * Establish relationships via phone calls or email marketing and conduct regular phone consultations with clients to advise them on how to get the best return on their Google Ads investment. * Own a portfolio of small businesses by thoroughly understanding growth drivers identifying opportunities for growth, managing risks and building quarterly plans for achievement. * Drive customer growth by delivering outstanding customer sales experience and achieving customer business and marketing objectives. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status.
