title: Investment Oversight Officer, Baillie Gifford Europe - 12 month fixed term contract
description: **Department** -------------- Operations Dublin\-International\-Ireland**Overview of Department** -------------------------- Baillie Gifford Investment Management (Europe) Limited (BGE), an AIFM \& UCITS Management Company based in Dublin and part of the wider Baillie Gifford Group, seeks an investment professional to serve as Investment Oversight Officer. **Purpose of Role** ------------------- As PCF30 (CIO) and PCF39D (Designated Person for Investment Management), you will be responsible for developing and maintaining a robust investment oversight framework across our UCITS funds, AIF and segregated mandates that ensures compliance with Central Bank of Ireland requirements and protects investor interests. You will provide strategic leadership for investment oversight activities and serve as the primary interface with delegated investment managers, regulators, and internal stakeholders.**Responsibilities** -------------------- * **Investment Oversight Leadership:** Maintain and enhance comprehensive investment oversight policies, procedures, and frameworks for all BGE funds and mandates in compliance with UCITS Directive, AIFMD, and Central Bank of Ireland requirements. Provide senior\-level oversight of the delegated investment manager, ensuring adherence to investment policies, risk parameters, and regulatory obligations. Lead quarterly and ad\-hoc due diligence reviews of delegated investment managers, assessing their capabilities, resources, and performance. Monitor and evaluate investment performance, risk metrics, portfolio positioning, and adherence to investment mandates across all funds. Oversee best execution compliance, and trading oversight frameworks. * **Regulatory and Compliance:** Serve as the designated person responsible for investment management under Central Bank of Ireland regulations. Ensure ongoing compliance with UCITS and AIFMD investment restrictions, risk limits, and operational requirements. Maintain current knowledge of evolving regulatory requirements and assess impact on BGE's investment oversight framework. * **Strategic and Business Development:** Lead Value for Money assessments and participate in fund launch initiatives, ensuring robust investment oversight frameworks are established from inception. Drive continuous improvement initiatives to enhance oversight effectiveness and operational efficiency. * **Reporting and Communication:** Prepare and present comprehensive investment oversight reports to the Board, Management Committee, and regulatory authorities when/if required. Communicate investment oversight findings, recommendations, and concerns to senior management and relevant committees. **Your knowledge and experience** * Experience in investment management, fund oversight, or related asset management functions and either currently or previously holding a PCF role or similar controlled functions in Ireland or other EU jurisdictions. * Demonstrated experience in senior investment oversight or investment management roles within UCITS and/or AIF environments and experience with regulatory interactions. * Strong understanding of Irish and EU regulatory frameworks including UCITS Directive, AIFMD, and Central Bank of Ireland requirements. * Understanding of investment strategies, portfolio construction, risk management, and performance measurement. * Strong analytical skills with experience in interpreting complex investment data and market information. * Project management capabilities and excellent written and verbal communication skills. **The type of candidate that we're looking for:** You will be an experienced investment oversight professional with strong stakeholder management skills and the ability to navigate regulatory environments. The ideal candidate will possess strong ethical standards and a firm commitment to investor protection. You should be a strategic thinker with a keen attention to detail and a focus on operational excellence. A collaborative leadership style is essential, along with the ability to influence across various organisational levels. Adaptability and resilience in a dynamic regulatory and market environment are crucial. Additionally, a commitment to continuous learning and professional development is expected. **Critical Skills** * Nurture relationships * Digital effectiveness (including AI) * Enabling others * Improvement mindset * Team working **Closing Date** ---------------- June 14, 2026*At Baillie Gifford we are committed to fostering an inclusive and respectful culture in which each of our colleagues can thrive and develop. We believe that our clients are best served by a diverse workforce with the experiences, ideas and perspectives that this brings.* If you are currently working at Baillie Gifford as an employee or contractor please apply to this job from the firm's Workday internal career site.

title: Japanese Speaking Technical Support Engineer
description: **Japanese\-Speaking Technical Support Engineer** At Dell Technologies, world\-class service doesn’t end when a customer purchases our innovative products. Our Technical Support team is there for them whenever they need help – by phone or email, online and on social media. No matter how complex the challenge, we rise to it. We answer questions about the installation, operation, configuration, customization and use of Dell Technologies products. For critical matters, we use our systems to provide remote diagnostic technical support. If there’s an issue with a customer’s product, we’ll analyze it, identify the cause, recommend a solution and document the problem. Join us to do the best work of your career and make a profound social impact as a Japanese\-speaking **Technical Support Engineer** on our **Technical Support Team** in **Cork** or **Dublin**. **What you’ll achieve** As a Technical Support Engineer, you will resolve complex technical issues in a fast\-paced, customer critical environment, working on improving processes, standards and strategies to perfect our Support Services. **You will:** * Resolve issues while working closely with multi\-functional teams. Troubleshoot and resolve complex hardware and software issues using diagnostic tools and technical expertise * Act as a customer advocate, communicating clear action plans and driving timely escalations. * Collaborate cross‑functionally with field, engineering, and local Japan teams, including during critical events. * Manage case priorities, maintain accurate documentation, and ensure consistent, closed‑loop communication. * Contribute to knowledge creation, and support continuous improvement of support processes. **Take the first step towards your dream career** Every Dell Technologies team member brings something unique to the table. Here’s what we are looking for with this role: **Essential Requirements** * Native‑level Japanese proficiency (spoken and written), plus strong English communication skills (spoken and written) * Ability to perform technical troubleshooting to identify issues and support resolution using diagnostic tools and technical knowledge * Ability to work under pressure with a strong customer focus and service mindset. Effective interpersonal and presentation skills for customer‑facing communication * Solid escalation judgment and ability to collaborate across teams to resolve issues. Solid previous experience in a customer service or customer‑facing support role * 2\+ years of Japanese customer support experience as an IT Infrastructure Support Engineer **Desirable Requirements** * Relevant technical certifications such as ISM v5, PowerStore/Unity Associate * PowerEdge Associate (LPIC‑1 or VCP‑DCV preferred) **Who we are** We believe that each of us has the power to make an impact. That’s why we put our team members at the center of everything we do. If you’re looking for an opportunity to grow your career with some of the best minds and most advanced tech in the industry, we’re looking for you. Dell Technologies is a unique family of businesses that helps individuals and organizations transform how they work, live and play. Join us to build a future that works for everyone because Progress Takes All of Us. **Application closing date:** Apply today! Dell Technologies is committed to the principle of equal employment opportunity for all employees and to providing employees with a work environment free of discrimination and harassment. Read the full Equal Employment Opportunity Policy here.

title: Junior Accounts Assistant
description: ### **Description** We are currently seeking a Junior Accounts Assistant to join our busy Finance Department on a fixed term contract for 12 months. The role will primarily be in Accounts Payable and will involve elements of Practice Management and other general offices duties. Training will be provided and will involve the use of various software and systems including Microsoft Dynamics GP, IRIS Practice Engine, SharePoint and Microsoft Office (particularly Excel and Outlook). This is an excellent opportunity for someone at the early stages of their career to develop their skills and gain valuable hands\-on experience within a busy finance department, supporting key activities as an Accounts Assistant.### **Key Responsibilities** *Accounts Payable** Purchase invoice cost centre analysis and data entry * Assisting in preparation of vendor payments * Reconciliation of vendor accounts *Practice Management** Analysis and recording of client outlays and disbursements * Review and analysis of staff expense claims *General Duties** Preparation of various general ledger journals * General filing, post etc ### **Skills, Knowledge and Expertise** * Good IT skills and knowledge of Microsoft applications (Excel and Outlook) * Ability to work on own initiative and to agreed reporting timelines * A proactive mindset with the ability to identify problems and propose solutions in a timely manner * Good time management and self\-motivation skills * A high level of discretion required * Some experience in an accounts payable environment is preferable but not essential ### **Benefits** *Office Facilities** State of the art high tech office in a Central Dublin City Centre location * Onsite gym, with changing \& shower facilities * Secure bike parking * Onsite café with complimentary barista *Health Benefits \&* *Leave Entitlements* * Digital Doctor access * Mini Health Assessments *Work Equipment \& Resources** Laptop * L\&D initiatives ### **About BDO Ireland** As part of the fifth largest global professional services firm, BDO stands as a leading force in Ireland's accountancy, tax, advisory, and consulting realm. Working with us fuels your career's purpose at every turn, offering unmatched breadth, quality, responsibility, and AI exposure. BDO Ireland is a key player in the international BDO network—the world's largest European\-led accounting group. With an excellent partner\-to\-staff ratio, our 91,054 experts collaborate with clients, delivering impactful advice from 1,658 locations across 167 countries. At BDO, we prioritise how we treat our people—colleagues and clients alike. Our international recognition for a mindful and compassionate company culture reflects our commitment to placing individuals at the heart of all we pursue.

title: Junior Business Analyst
description: **Want to make an impact? We offer that.** Due to continued growth, we are currently hiring a **Junior Business Analyst** to join our team. emagine is a dynamic, fast\-growing international consulting and technology company, founded on real\-world experience and focused on delivering custom\-fit solutions. We have the proven people and expertise to complete complex projects while delivering lasting knowledge\-transfer benefits to empower our partners for the next challenge. This opportunity will suit a candidate with experience in **application support, business analysis, reporting, system configuration, and data migration activities** within a structured delivery environment. **Are you intrigued? Do you want to learn more?** A snapshot of your key responsibilities as a Junior Business Analyst would be: * Provide Level 1 and Level 2 application support to internal and external stakeholders * Diagnose, troubleshoot, and resolve system issues while managing support tickets and ensuring SLA compliance * Support user onboarding, training, and creation of support documentation * Assist with the design, configuration, testing, and deployment of system enhancements and new workflows * Participate in User Acceptance Testing (UAT) and rollout activities * Support data migration activities, including validation, reconciliation, testing, and quality assurance processes * Develop and maintain functional and technical documentation * Analyse existing business processes and identify opportunities for improvement * Act as a liaison between business users and technical delivery teams * Support development and enhancement of Power BI dashboards and management reporting * Provide technical support for dashboards and reporting solutions Requirements: * Degree\-level qualification in a relevant discipline such as Information Technology, Computer Science, Project Management, Finance, or equivalent * 1–3 years’ relevant experience demonstrating progressive responsibility * Experience working with SmartSimple (highly desirable) or similar enterprise platforms * Proven experience delivering L1/L2 application support processes in a live production environment * Demonstrated IT Business Analyst experience, ideally within public sector or regulated environments * Experience supporting systems implementation and configuration activities * Experience supporting data migration projects, including testing and validation activities * Knowledge of Power BI reporting and dashboard development * Strong analytical and problem\-solving capability * Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills * Excellent verbal and written English So, are you ready to join our team? It’s important to remember, **emagine is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer** ; we value bringing together individuals from diverse backgrounds to develop innovative solutions for our customers. **Opportunity type:** Contract, Full\-time, Hybrid ( **2 days onsite / 3 days remote** ) **Start date:** August 2026

title: Junior Chef de Partie - City centre
description: Are you a talented culinary enthusiast with a passion for creating delicious dishes? **Gourmet Food Parlour** need you! As a Junior Chef de Partie,in our restaurant in the renowned **Arnotts** department store. You'll be an essential member of our kitchen team, working in your designated section to prepare and present mouthwatering meals. We're looking for someone who can bring their culinary skills and creativity to the table while maintaining a fun and friendly kitchen atmosphere. If you're ready to showcase your cooking talents and be a part of a fantastic kitchen brigade, then this is the role for you! **Duties and Responsibilities:** * Culinary Expertise: Utilize your cooking skills to prepare and cook a variety of dishes in your assigned kitchen section. * Section Ownership: Take pride in managing your kitchen station, ensuring that everything runs smoothly and efficiently. * Tasty Presentations: Plate dishes with care and attention, making sure they look as good as they taste. * Team Collaboration: Work as part of our kitchen team, supporting your fellow chefs to deliver outstanding service. * Quality Assurance: Maintain our high standards of taste and presentation, consistently delivering top\-notch dishes. * Freshness Focus: Pay attention to ingredient quality and freshness to ensure the best possible flavors. * Continuous Learning: Be open to learning new techniques and recipes to grow as a culinary professional. * Time Management: Keep up with the pace of our kitchen during busy periods, ensuring orders are served on time. * Clean Kitchen, Happy Kitchen: Help keep the kitchen clean and organized for a safe and pleasant working environment. **Qualifications and Skills:** * Culinary Passion: A genuine love for cooking and a desire to create exceptional dining experiences. * Kitchen Experience: Previous experience as a line cook or similar position is preferred, but a passion for cooking and a willingness to learn are most important. * Attention to Detail: Paying attention to the little things can make a big difference in the quality of your dishes. * Team Player: Working together and supporting your colleagues is vital in our kitchen culture. * Adaptability: Being flexible and able to handle different tasks and challenges is key in a bustling kitchen. * Food Safety Awareness: In\-Depth knowledge of food safety practices to ensure a clean and safe kitchen environment. * Pizza experience desired but not a requirement. **Requirements** * HACCP trained to level 2 minimum * Minimum 1 year experience cooking in a busy kitchen * Fluent English * Full time * Available to work flexible hours that may include early mornings, evenings, weekends, nights and/or holidays * Meet store operating policies and standards, including providing quality food products, meeting HACCP guidelines and store safety and security. * Comply with a strict dress and personal hygiene code that meets HACCP guidelines Why join our team? * Flexible work environment * Training \& Career progression * Arnott's staff discount * Access to our dedicated Training platform * Employee assist program * Full time position Join our culinary family and let's make magic happen in the kitchen together! As a Chef de Partie, you'll have the chance to create delicious dishes and be part of a supportive team that values your culinary talent and creativity. So, bring your passion for cooking, and let's cook up something special! Gourmet Food Parlour is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We are committed to providing an environment of mutual respect where equal employment opportunities are available to all applicants.

title: Junior Planner
description: **Are you looking to become part of a collaborative team whilst maximising your potential to grow and develop within your career? Apply today.** **The Role.** GMC are currently looking for a **Junior** **Planner** to join our growing team in Dublin. The successful Planner will be responsible for developing, maintaining, and monitoring project programmes across a range of infrastructure and utilities works, ensuring projects are delivered safely, on time, and within budget. Experience working under NEC contracts is highly desirable. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** **Key Responsibilities:** Develop, manage, and update detailed project programmes using Primavera P6 and/or MS Project Work closely with Project Managers, Engineers, and Commercial teams to produce realistic and achievable schedules Monitor project progress, identify risks and delays, and propose mitigation measures Prepare short\-term and look\-ahead programmes Carry out programme impact and delay analysis where required Support change management and compensation events (particularly under NEC contracts) Attend project and progress meetings, providing planning updates and reports Ensure programmes align with construction methodologies, procurement schedules, and resource plans Assist in the preparation of tender programmes and planning inputs for bids Maintain accurate planning records and reporting dashboards **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** Degree or relevant qualification in Engineering, Construction Management, or a related discipline 1\+ years experience as a Planner within Civils and/or Utilities projects Strong knowledge of construction sequencing and methodologies Proficiency in Primavera P6 and/or MS Project **NEC Contract Experience Is Preferred** Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and communication skills Ability to work collaboratively with multidisciplinary teams Excellent attention to detail and time management skills **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. GMC is an equal opportunity employer, and we aim to recruit and retain like\-minded people from diverse backgrounds with a wide range of expertise. If you require any specific accommodations, we will make every reasonable effort to support you throughout the recruitment and selection process. Please contact us at careers@gmcirl.com. In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. We have engaged with our staff and other stakeholders to agree and publish our core values. One of our core values is to *"Focus on the Future"* . Our growth to date reflects the confidence that our customers have in our service delivery, in the innovative approach that we bring to how we do our work, and to our culture of safety and quality. We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.

title: Lawyer
description: **Job Title:** Lawyer **Department:** Legal **Contract Type:** Permanent **Location:** Dublin, Ireland (Hybrid) **Why work with us?** We are the bank for a changing world and we aim to build positive change together: * We are a team of colleagues who are enthusiastic about what we do and the impact our work has on our clients. We work in a highly collaborative, open\-minded environment that embraces innovation and creativity * We offer competitive remuneration and a comprehensive benefits package, including a discretionary bonus scheme, company pension contribution, comprehensive private healthcare packages, health and fitness subsidy, work\-from\-home subsidy, above statutory days of annual leave and numerous other benefits. * We are strongly committed to wellbeing and work\-life balance, including a 50% hybrid work model (where the role allows), paid family leave, fertility and menopause support — available to support all life stage needs. * Equality, diversity and inclusion are at the core of our culture, and we actively promote equal opportunities through creating or being part of initiatives and programmes: * Aspire Female Leadership Programme, provides development and exposure to high\-potential women in the bank, through targeted workshops, executive coaching and formal mentoring * Proud members of 100 Women in Finance and among the first signatories of Ireland’s Women in Finance charter * Joined the \#JamaisSansElles (Never Without Her) Charter, committing its executive committee to only join public or media events with panels of three or more speakers, if at least one of those speakers is a woman * Development and mobility are actively encouraged. BNP Paribas’ culture is built on purposeful talent development and recognition programmes that foster engagement, inclusive leadership and continuous growth. A few of our programmes include: * Leaders for Tomorrow, a programme that fast\-tracks high\-potential talent through training, development and cross\-functional projects, preparing them for future leadership positions * Local and Global Mentoring initiatives, that pair employees with senior leaders, providing staff with platforms to gain advice on career growth topics * Ambassador Awards employee recognition scheme provides rewards to those who have embodied the Bank’s core values * Local Experts (Champions) Programme, identifying internal subject\-matter experts from specific business areas, who excel at their craft * We are a global company operating in over 60 countries with over 180,000 employees from over 150 nationalities. In Ireland we have approximately 550 employees across Dublin and Galway, working in Corporate Institutional Banking (CIB) and BNP Paribas Security Services (BP2S) and Greenval **Equality and Diversity** BNP Paribas is an equal opportunities employer and proudly cultivates a diverse workforce. We believe that diversity of experience enhances our service offering to clients and leads to a better working environment. We welcome applications from the best candidates regardless of age, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, nationality, disability, sexual orientation, socio\-economic background, parental and caring status, or religious belief. Our Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) play a key role in promoting diversity and inclusion through initiatives, events and advocacy programmes that foster a sense of belonging for all colleagues. In BNP Paribas Ireland, we have dedicated networks for parents and caregivers, LGBTQA\+, staff from diverse cultural and religious backgrounds, alongside forums to discuss CSR and gender related topics. **Sustainable Finance** BNP continues to demonstrate the Bank’s steadfast commitment to supporting its clients in their growth ambitions by expanding market reach across assets, delivering excellence in bond markets, and showing the way in sustainable financing. **Role** The Dublin Legal team (CIB), comprises the Head of Legal and 6 other lawyers, a company secretary and support staff. It serves a broad spread of CIB businesses with a strong focus on supply chain management and Transaction Banking (including corporate lending and flow banking products such as Cash Management). We require an Irish or English qualified lawyer on a full\-time basis to join the legal team to support the growing business lines and various functions in the territory. **Key Responsibilities** * Participation in the Financing Legal Platform, including project and transactional support as required. * Support the Supply Chain Management (“SCM”) Group through each of the SCM teams around the world, particularly Dublin, Paris and London, in documenting their transactions and developing robust structures. * Working on the Payable and Receivable structures including analyses of novel electronic platform solutions for the GTS Supply Chain Team. * Working on the implementation of any Bank led projects * Monitoring any developments in legislation and identification of legal risk in the territory and reporting into the SARA tool. * Answering ad hoc queries on NDA’s or any Account opening or onboardings. * Assisting Compliance with sanctions papering and or escalations. * Advising on day to day general corporate law matters, primarily corporate governance queries, arising from the Bank’s activity and the Bank’s subsidiaries activities. * Queries on deposit and current accounts and any new investment and loan products, including marketing materials, if required. * Contributing to IP/IT legal matters that may be allocated to you. * Liaising with business colleagues within the global banking network in the structuring, drafting, negotiation and closing of transactions for the Bank. * Supporting commercial banking activities including bilateral and syndicated lending and trade services offerings. * Working closely with the global trade services team from a legal perspective, including the drafting and review of supply chain finance transactions, working with third title: Marketing Internship 2026.
description: At AIB, our values guide how we work and how we support each other. We’re looking for someone who puts **Customer First** , takes initiative and **Owns the Outcome** , and is always looking for ways to **Eliminate Complexity** . You’ll treat colleagues and customers with fairness and **Show Respect** , and you’ll thrive in a culture built on collaboration where we **Be One Team** to deliver meaningful impact. **Location/Office Policy:** 5 days a week, with 3 in\-person in our office in Central Park, Leopardstown, Dublin. **Duration:** This is a 12 month internship * Do you want to take the first step towards building a career in Marketing, with a particular focus on sponsorship? * Are you obsessed with designing amazing customer experiences? * Do you love seeing something come to life that you were involved with right from the brief? **What Is The Role** **Working on a dynamic and fast paced team in Group Marketing, the candidate will be required to:** * Work with partners, agencies, rights\-holders and colleagues to provide world class sponsorship and experiences for our customers * Take responsibility for completing tasks and contribute to problem solving * Have a passion for marketing, particularly sponsorship, and be dedicated to building world\-class experiences that will amaze our customers We’re one of Ireland’s biggest banks, and we have a passionate marketing team who are customer obsessed. We are a function that is made of small teams where you have the chance to shine, whether it is understanding customers insights, developing experiences that wow our customers or ensuring we are reaching our customers in the right way. In each team you’ll find leaders who focus as much on people as they do on function. Here, anyone who is responsible for managing people is called a people leader, not a manager. That shows our commitment and focus on the development and engagement of the people who work here. You’ll find a mix of age, race and gender, where everyone has an opportunity to reach their potential. You’ll find a culture of openness, positivity and respect where everyone has a voice. We are looking for the best experienced people as well as graduates. We will match your energy and enthusiasm with empowerment so that together we can build a culture that breaks the conventions of what our customer and employees expect of a bank. **Key Accountabilities** * Own the end‑to‑end coordination and delivery of assigned sponsorship and experiential activities * Maintain, and manage controls on team budgets with PO responsibility * Act as a central point of liaison across internal stakeholders and external partners * Proactively update internal registers with group\-wide reach to ensure they are correct * Coordinate operational elements in support of projects e.g. event logistics, stakeholder communications, content drafts, and asset management * Support the broader team as needed with lots of potential for on\-the\-job learning beyond the role **What You Will Bring** * Initiative and accountability in executing the tasks set to the highest standards * Effective communication skills which can flex to interact with colleagues, stakeholders and partners * Be a strong team player and understand the importance of working with others * Focus on measurement and effectiveness **Why Work For AIB** We are committed to offering our colleagues choice and flexibility in how we work and live and our hybrid working model enables our people to balance their time between working from home and their designated office, subject to their role, the needs of our customers and business requirements. **Some Of Our Benefits Include** * Market leading Pension Scheme * Healthcare Scheme * Variable Pay * Employee Assistance Programme * Family leave options * Two volunteer days per year Please click **here** for further information about AIB’s PACT – Our Commitment to You. **Key Capabilities** * Customer Focus – Keeping customer front of mind and developing customer centric solutions * Collaborates \- Building partnerships and working collaboratively with others to meet shared objectives. * Ensures Accountability \- Holding self and others accountable to meet commitments. If you are not sure about your suitability based on any aspects of the role advertised, we encourage you to please contact the recruiter for this role, Sophie Doherty, at careers@aib.ie for a conversation. AIB is an equal opportunities employer, and we pride ourselves on being the first bank in Ireland to receive the Investors in Diversity Gold Standard accreditation from the Irish Centre for Diversity. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations for applicants and employees. Should you have a reasonable accommodation request please email the Talent Acquisition team at careers@aib.ie **Disclaimer** Unsolicited CV’s sent to AIB by Recruitment Agencies will not be accepted for this position. AIB operates a direct sourcing model and where agency assistance is required, the Talent Acquisition team will engage directly with our recruitment partners. **Application deadline : Friday 29th of May**

title: Mechanical Supervisor
description: **Position: Mechanical Maintenance Site Supervisor** **Location: Onsite – Dublin, National Children’s Hospital** **Company Description** We are a leading engineering services company with a proven track record of high quality, customer focused project delivery for over 130 years. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. Some of the other services we provide include Waste to Energy (BioEnergy), Specialist Lift, Off Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling) and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. **Job Description** As a Site Supervisor, you will play a pivotal role in our project development and implementation process by reporting to the Technical Services Manager on all aspects of the maintenance/small works operations. You will work closely with the Project Manager as well as a multidisciplinary team of engineers and industry experts to deliver cutting\-edge maintenance services in line with HTM and SFG20\. Current requirements are onsite based in a state\-of\-the\-art healthcare facility in Dublin City. Your responsibilities will include: * Ensure all site operations are carried out in strict compliance with company HSQE requirements and standards. * Daily management, supervision, and reporting, of direct labour resources, plant, safe execution of works and materials allocations. * Manage and coordinate sub\-contractors on site. * Liaise with BIM engineer to update construction models following all upgrade works. * Carry out company compliance and administration procedures and recording performance and progress of site operations and subcontractors. * Review of work packages to ensure all necessary information \& permits are in place to execute the works safely and efficiently. This role will provide exposure to a wide range of expertise relating to healthcare, construction, and facilities sectors. The successful candidate will be supported in developing their knowledge and skills as their responsibilities grow and evolve. **Skills and Requirements** To excel in this role, you should possess the following skills and qualifications: * 2 years’ experience in a site lead role working in a construction or maintenance team with a strong emphasis on Safety and Quality. * Minimum Level 6 of the QQI Certificate in Mechanical Craft Practice or equivalent * Demonstrable a high level of technical knowledge with practical application. * A high degree of computer literacy. **Interested parties should apply here and/or mail colinodonnell@joneseng.com** **Benefits** We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our company values work\-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programs. If you are ready to make a difference and be part of a growing industry, please submit your CV, along with a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and why you are interested in this role. Recognising the contributions and respecting our people is core to our culture and values. We are an equal opportunities employer, and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for roles. Please note. We are currently not accepting applications from agencies, for this role at this time. **About Us** Joining our team means joining our legacy. At Jones Engineering we have over 130 years’ experience as a world class engineering contractor. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. \#IJA

title: Network Cabling Quality Technician
description: **DESCRIPTION** --------------- AWS Infrastructure Services (AIS) designs, plans, delivers, and operates all AWS global infrastructure. We keep the cloud running by supporting AWS data centers and the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that give customers continual access to innovation. We solve complex problems with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain. You'll join software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other essential roles. You'll collaborate across AWS to deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing capacity at the lowest cost for customers. In this role, you will: Work with Network Deployment and Vendor Management teams to integrate quality assurance into cable installation processes throughout the project lifecycle. You'll participate in reviews of training curriculums and cabling design documents, providing Quality Assurance representation to ensure standards are met during cable installation activities. You'll influence partner teams and cabling vendors to adopt quality assurance checklists that validate conformance to both Amazon and jurisdictional standards during critical milestone checks for cable installation and testing activities. You'll bring the voice of the customer forward by gathering feedback from internal stakeholders and cabling vendors, then refining quality processes based on their needs. You'll verify that cable installations meet standards by reviewing documentation and data from milestone inspections and cable validation testing. You'll drive cable validation testing adoption and defect management while establishing performance benchmarks to improve vendor performance and enhance quality metrics. You'll document and align cable installation standards and standard operating procedures as new technology and cabling methods integrate into our infrastructure. You’ll also conduct training with partner teams and provide guidelines to cabling vendors to ensure consistent execution across all network installations. This role matters because cable installation quality directly impacts data center network reliability and customer experience. You'll own the quality process from participating in cabling design reviews through training delivery, ensuring our infrastructure meets the standards our customers depend on. AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help. You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion. Key job responsibilities * Support Quality Assurance related programs to drive world\-class quality in network cabling in the new constructions of Data Centers to achieve the best operating, lowest cost infrastructure in the world. The Network Cabling Quality Technician will be obsessed with quality, engineering and design continuous improvements * Partner with Network Deployment teams to adopt defined quality assurance checklists, gather feedback, and generate recommendations to improve checklists. Work with partner teams to track cabling scope changes and perform impact analysis to address business needs * Partner with the Vendor Management team to drive cable vendor quality, provide vendor performance metrics through business reviews, and refine processes to improve vendor performance * Support cable installation benchmark according to Amazon policies and industry standards; maintain cable installation standards, checklists, standard operating procedures and best practices guidelines * Conduct Network Cabling Installation Quality Training Programs, and the creation of training materials, to support internal and external partner and stakeholder teams * Collaborate with stakeholder teams to capture metrics data such as inspection reports and cable test results; track defects for root cause analysis, generate recommendations, evaluate vendor performances and escalate issues to respective owners for remediation plans * Track and report on project milestones, vendor performance metrics, and quality KPIs including First Pass Yield, defect rates, and schedule adherence across multiple concurrent projects * Support continuous improvement initiatives by capturing lessons learned, facilitating post\-project reviews, and implementing process improvements to enhance future project delivery * Identify opportunities to reduce Cost of Poor Quality and improve overall project efficiency * Regional and Local Travel up to 25% to attend site visits and meetings for onsite quality standards review and collect feedback from onsite personnel A day in the life Amazon Web Services (AWS) is a highly innovative company that collaborates across disciplines to produce new technology that changes our world. The AWS Quality Team is a fast\-growing global team that is rethinking the implementation and design for the critical arteries that connect the various pieces of hardware inside AWS’ data centers and edge sites. As a member of this team, you will be charged with visiting our data centers and ensuring that we are following AWS and Industry standards while also collecting lessons learned so we are continually improving. You will work directly with engineers, managers, and operations teams across the company to develop and improve network pathway and installation standards based on these observations and lessons learned. Inclusive Team Culture Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee\-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness. AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, , and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. title: Network Engineer

title: Occupational Therapist
description: ### **Description** **Join Our Team as a Full\-Time Occupational Therapist at The Marlay.** Are you a dedicated and passionate Occupational Therapist looking for a rewarding role across Nursing Home services? CareChoice is expanding our Allied Health team, and we are now inviting applications for full\-time Occupational Therapist position in The Marlay. **Why Choose CareChoice?** At CareChoice, we are committed to delivering exceptional, person\-centred care across our network of facilities. With over two decades of experience and a growing portfolio of specialist rehabilitation and residential services, we are continually developing innovative models of care to support residents and patients with diverse and complex needs. Join us as we expand our multidisciplinary teams and strengthen our clinical services across the CareChoice Group. **Responsibilities:** * Be responsible for managing own caseload and for assessment, planning, implementation and evaluation of treatment/ intervention programmes for service users according to service standards * Assessments to include but are not limited to seating assessments, complete functional assessments, complete hand assessments, cognitive assessments and recommendations on environmental layout. * Collaborate with service users, family, carers and other staff in treatment/intervention planning and in the provision of support and advice. * Plan discharge or transition of the service user between services as appropriate. * Ensure the quality of documentation of all assessments, treatment plans, progress notes, reports and discharge summaries are in accordance with professional standards * Foster and maintain professional working relationships with colleagues, front\-line managers, and other healthcare personnel in the team * Be involved in service planning and development by anticipating the changing needs of the service and service users for the inpatient service * Demonstrate a commitment to research and evidence\-based practice, identifying and contributing to research, clinical evaluation and audit opportunities * Document, and communicate verbally and / or in writing results of assessments, treatment/intervention programmes and recommendations to the team and relevant others in accordance with service policy * Arrange and carry out duties in a timely manner within settings appropriate to service users' needs and in line with local policy/guidelines. Attend review meetings, team meetings, case conferences, ward rounds etc. * Maintain quality standards of practice and participate in quality assurance and clinical audits as appropriate. * Be responsible for adhering to existing standards and protocols and for leading out on the development and maintenance of standards/strategies for quality improvement and outcome measurement * Incorporate national and international Evidence\-Based Occupational Therapy Practice (EBOTP) into their practice * Ensure that professional standards are maintained in relation to confidentiality, ethics and legislation * Operate within the scope of Occupational Therapy practice within Ireland and in accordance with local guidelines * Adhere to the Ethics for Occupational Therapists from CORU and the AOTI. **Eligibility criteria:** * Hold a recognised qualification BSc or MSc as an Occupational Therapist and have validation of your qualifications if you qualified outside the Republic of Ireland. * 2 year minimum, post\-graduate experience as an Occupational Therapist. * Registration with CORU. * Must have their own vehicle and full driving licence. * Excellent language and communication skills as accurate communication is essential. * Good interpersonal skills are essential. * All applicants must be legally entitled to work in Ireland.

title: Operations & Brand Intern, Summer @ Mafia Matcha
description: ***First filter: you already drink matcha. Like, properly. You have a tin at home, you have opinions about it, and you’ve definitely paid €6 for one in town and not regretted it. If that’s not you, this one isn’t for you!*** We’re Mafia Matcha \- an Irish matcha brand now stocked in over 200 coffee shops and retailers across Ireland, plus our own DTC channel and pop\-ups around Dublin. We’re a small team building something a bit louder, a bit more fun, and a lot better\- tasting than what’s already out there \- and we’re growing fast enough that the founder genuinely needs a second pair of hands across ops and brand this summer. This isn’t a “shadow the team and write a report at the end” internship. You’ll be in the warehouse packing real orders, in the office throwing ideas around, and out at pop\-ups talking matcha with strangers \- often on the same day. **What a week actually looks like:** One day you’re at our Sandyford warehouse packing 30 orders, restocking, and making sure the shelves don’t descend into chaos. The next you’re at the office helping us figure out which 15 creators to send PR boxes to, drafting the outreach, and getting the boxes out the door. Saturday you might be at a pop\-up in town whisking matcha for people who’ve never tried it and convincing them they’ve been missing out. Monday you bring back three ideas from what you saw at the pop\-up and we actually try one of them. That’s the job. Ops \+ brand \+ customer\-facing, mixed together, every week different. **The split, roughly** * \~50% Operations \- packing orders, warehouse organisation, stock, the day\-to\-day of a small brand running properly * \~30% Brand \& PR \- running our PR/UGC outreach end\-to\-end: building lists, writing outreach, prepping boxes, following up, learning what works * \~20% Pop\-ups \& events \- repping the brand in person, serving matcha, educating people, being the face of Mafia Matcha out in the wild The split flexes week to week. Some weeks are heavier on events, some on ops. Some weekend availability is needed \- pop\-ups happen when our customers are free, which is often Saturdays. You’ll thrive here if you: * Are a 2nd or 3rd year Business\-oriented student (marketing, commerce, entrepreneurship, management \- anything in that family) * Genuinely drink matcha and can talk about it without faking it * Are obsessed with brands and social \- you follow visual brands, you screenshot good packaging, you have opinions about why some DTC brands fly and others die * Bring ideas without being asked. If you notice the order labels could be laid out better, you say so. If a creator we should DM isn\&\#39;t on our list, you add them. * Are organised enough that nothing slips, and chilled enough to roll with a startup week where the plan changes on Wednesday * Are warm and confident with strangers at pop\-ups \- not performative, just genuinely happy to chat about something you love * Have your own car \- non\-negotiable. You\&\#39;ll be moving between Sandyford and pop\-ups around Dublin with equipment. * Live in South Dublin \- Dundrum, Stillorgan, Sandyford, Foxrock, Blackrock, Dún Laoghaire etc. Max \~20 min by car from Sandyford. **This isn’t the job for you if:** * You don’t drink matcha and you’re hoping you’ll “grow into it” * You don’t drive or don’t have access to a car * You want a structured corporate internship with a defined programme and a manager telling you what to do every hour * The idea of packing orders or doing manual warehouse work feels beneath you (we all do it, including the founders) * You’re not based in or near South Dublin and the commute would be a stretch * “Just doing what’s on your job description” is your default setting **Logistics:** * Pay: €16,5/hour \- paid for every hour worked, including weeks that run longer * Hours: 20/week base, some weeks more with prior agreement * Start: Monday 22nd June 2026 * End: Sunday 14th September 2026, with strong possibility of extension into a part\-time role through the academic year (hours flexed around your timetable). That’s the actual goal here, we’d rather invest in one person who sticks for another year or two than re\-hire every summer. * Location: Sandyford\-based, with pop\-ups around Dublin and beyond * Application deadline: 9th June 2026 **How to apply:** Send the following to jobs@mafiamatcha.com: 1\. Your CV (one page, please) 2\. A short intro \- written, voice note, or Loom, whatever you prefer. Tell us why Mafia Matcha specifically, and your honest matcha story (when, how often, favourite way to drink it). 3\. Pick one: a) Name 3 creators or press contacts you’d want us to send a PR box to, and one line on why each b) Tell us one thing about Mafia Matcha’s social / brand / website you’d change, and what you’d try instead Follow us before you apply: @mafiamatcha IG/TikTok \- it’ll tell you more about who we are than any job ad can:)

title: Operations Supervisor - Mechanical / environmental background

title: People Consultant
description: Who we are **About Stripe** Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. **About The Team** One of the key drivers to our success is an exceptional emphasis on our people. We believe that our competitive advantage lies in our talent and that we can build a sustainable advantage by enabling individuals and teams to contribute their full potential. The People Consultant team helps to drive those efforts across our Tech, Core, and Business Organizations. This includes supporting our leaders and managers through scale, creating an environment where Stripes of all backgrounds can thrive, aligning our business strategy with our people strategy, using data to develop best practices from first principles. We are committed to building an inclusive work environment where all Stripes feel welcomed as their authentic selves—inclusive of all genders, sexual orientations, ethnicities, races, education, ages, and other personal characteristics. We work on broadening our internal diversity because we want to be culturally equipped to build products and solutions for our diverse user base. What you’ll do As a People Consultant, you’ll help make Stripe a great place to work and grow. You’ll work with business leaders, managers, and employees across Stripe to strategize and implement systems, processes, and programs to help people succeed. **Responsibilities** * Partner with and advise front line managers on performance management, coaching, and employee relations. Collaborate closely with Employee Relations and Legal teams to foster sound business decisions. * Work with the Legal team and external consultants to help ensure Stripe’s compliance with local labor regulations * Advise and consult front line management on Stripe People policies and best practices, in collaboration with fellow People team members and counsel as necessary * Partner directly with People Partners (HRBPs), managers, and leaders on wider organizational strategy and initiatives, including reorganizations * Facilitate smooth roll out of key People programs, including front line manager support for performance review cycles, compensation cycles, employee engagement surveys and ad hoc programs * Analyze and present on organizational health trends and insights to managers and leaders; manage tracking and reporting of some key people data to inform team strategy * Ensure excellent employee experience through key life cycle stages including the departure processes Who you are We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. **Minimum Requirements** * Minimum of 3\+ years of HR experience; at least 1 year in a client facing HR role (e.g. HR Business Partner, Employee Relations partner) * Proven track record of partnering directly with managers on performance management, coaching, and resolving sensitive employee relations issues * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills demonstrated through success diagnosing problems using data, reasoning clearly and determining viable solutions, and remaining flexible in those solutions’ implementation * Experience utilizing company policies and practices to determine appropriate recommendations for action * Exceptional communication skills, both written and verbal **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience working in a fast paced, growing environment, preferably in a technology company or a large scale organization * Project management skills, with an ability to design and execute clear processes while successfully engaging peers and stakeholders to deliver work on schedule * Proven ability to identify areas for improvement and escalate recurring themes to key decision makers when needed In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in\-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible. Pay and benefits The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is €88,000 \- €132,000\. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.

title: Physical Security Engineer
description: **Overview** **Business Function Overview:** In alignment with our Microsoft values, we are committed to cultivating an inclusive work environment for all employees to positively impact our culture every day and we need you as a **Physical Security Engineer** . Microsoft’s Cloud Operations \& Innovation (CO\+I) is the engine that powers our cloud services. As a **Physical Security Engineer** , you will perform a key role in delivering \& sustaining physical security infrastructure and foundational technologies for Microsoft's online services including CoPilot, Bing, Office 365, Xbox, OneDrive, and the Microsoft Azure platform. This will include managing the health of physical security systems across a portfolio of locations through break/fix operations, participating in on\-call DRI rotation to provide support, ensuring that systems \& hardware are configured in alignment with baseline standards, participate in program improvements in partnership with other teams, and enabling the delivery of new datacenter capacity through programming access control and video systems. This is a high visibility position in an area of large and expanding investment for Microsoft and offers a terrific opportunity for technical and career growth. This is a flexible work opportunity role offering remote work from home or in person work as desired. We are focused on the personal and professional development for all employees and offer trainings and growth opportunities including Career Rotation Programs, Diversity \& Inclusion trainings and events, and professional certifications. Microsoft’s Cloud Operations \& Innovation (CO\+I) is the engine that powers our cloud services. As a CE Platform Engineer you will perform a key role in delivering the core infrastructure and foundational technologies for Microsoft's online services including Bing, Office 365, Xbox, OneDrive, and the Microsoft Azure platform. As a group, CO\+I is focused on the personal and professional development for all employees and offers trainings and growth opportunities including Career Rotation Programs, Diversity \& Inclusion trainings and events, and professional certifications. Our infrastructure is comprised of a large global portfolio of more than 100 datacenters and 1 million servers. Our foundation is built upon and managed by a team of subject matter experts working to support services for more than 1 billion customers and 20 million businesses in over 90 countries worldwide. With environmental sustainability and optimization at the forefront of our datacenter design and operations, we continue to grow and evolve as we meet the ever\-changing business demands that hold Microsoft as a world\-class cloud provider. Do you want to empower billions across the world? Come and join us in CO\+I and be at the forefront of the action! **Responsibilities** **Physical Security System Implementation** * Program physical access control and video systems as part of the delivery of new datacenters. * Provide oversight of security systems design for access control and video programming to ensure compliance with design standards and requirements. **Maintaining and Operating Physical Security Systems** * Participate in on\-call DRI rotation to support physical security systems. * Assess data related to the health of security systems and infrastructure to identify gaps, performance trends, compliance with system configuration standards, and work with peers to recommend and execute repeatable strategies to address issues at scale. * Continuously identify and recommend improvements across the program to processes, procedures, and documentation. * Manage physical security systems lifecycle support and compliance, hosting regular datacenter site meetings in partnership with security operations and facility management teams to proactively address site security system health, and coordinate maintenance and repair services with internal and external service providers. **Collaboration And Partner Experience** * Collaborate with partner engineering, project management, and design teams on initiatives that help to improve the physical security environment and improve the overall effectiveness of the program. **Other** * Embody our culture and values. **Qualifications** **Required Qualifications:** * Relevant Experience in physical security, large\-scale computing, modeling, cyber security, anomaly detection, Security Operations Center (SOC) detection, threat analytics, security incident, and event management (SIEM), information technology (IT), and operations incident response. * Bachelor's Degree in Electronic or Electrical Engineering, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science or related field OR equivalent experience. **Background Check Requirements** Ability to meet Microsoft, customer and/or government security screening requirements are required for this role. These requirements include, but are not limited to the following specialized security screenings: * Microsoft Cloud Background Check: This position will be required to pass the Microsoft Cloud background check upon hire/transfer and every two years thereafter. **Preferred Qualifications** * Lenel Certifications, Milestones Certifications, CISSP, CISA, CISM, SANS, GCIA, GCIH, OSCP, and/or Security\+ certification. This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled. Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance with religious accommodations and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process, read more about **requesting accommodations.**

title: Platform Architect
description: **Platform Architect** **Fulltime \- Permanent position** **Hybrid 2 days onsite based in Dublin city center.** Our High Profile Client seeks a Platform Architect to lead the design, development, and management of its Cloud Azure platform and DevOps. This hands\-on role requires deep expertise in Azure or AWS, DevOps, and cloud\-native technologies, supporting client’s digital transformation and growth strategy **Key Responsibilities** * Own and drive the roadmap, capabilities, and governance of client’s Azure\-based DevOps and Cloud Platform. * Analyse, design, and optimise DevOps and cloud engineering practices across teams. * Implement automation solutions using Azure DevOps, Jenkins, Terraform, and Azure\-native services. * Champion best practices in software development, cloud operations, IaC, CI/CD, observability, cost management, and security. * Establish and maintain CI/CD environments to accelerate delivery and platform evolution. * Provide expert guidance to development, platform, and operations teams throughout the project lifecycle. * Monitor and manage technical operations and platform reliability using Azure\-native tools (e.g., Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, Defender for Cloud). * Providing expertise to ensure solutions are scalable, reliable, secure, and compliant, aligned with enterprise architecture. * Develop reusable patterns, reference architectures, and automation frameworks to standardise platform usage. **Essential Skills \& Experience** * 5\+ years’ experience in systems/solutions architecture, with strong exposure to cloud\-native and hybrid enterprise environments. * Deep architectural knowledge of Azure Cloud Platform, including core services, PaaS, Azure DevOps, Landing Zones, and governance. * Strong knowledge of CI/CD pipelines (preferably Azure DevOps; Jenkins also valuable). * Hands\-on experience with Infrastructure as Code (Terraform) for Azure resource deployment. * Experience with artefact management, release engineering, and automated software delivery. * Good knowledge of cloud networking (VNETs, private endpoints, load balancers, DNS, firewalls, hybrid connectivity). * Systems integration experience across multi\-vendor and multi\-cloud platforms. * Working knowledge of at least one API Gateway (Azure API Management preferred). * Experience with container platforms (Kubernetes, AKS, Docker). * Familiarity with Atlassian toolset (JIRA, Confluence) * Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, able to explain complex concepts to diverse audiences. * Experience mentoring technical staff and guiding teams through cloud transformation. * Strong problem\-solving skills, attention to detail, and ability to manage multiple workstreams. * Fluent in English (written and verbal). **Desired Skills \& Experience** * Experience with container orchestration (Kubernetes/AKS, Docker Swarm). * Experience with Database DevOps/DB CI/CD tooling (e.g., Liquibase). * Knowledge of the insurance or financial services industry. * Working knowledge of German an asset. * Degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or related discipline.

title: Porter

As a Porter, you will use your skills to maintain a high standard of customer care and cleanliness. In return, you will have the chance to progress your career with a company that invests in its people, celebrates individuality, and rewards and recognises employees who go beyond the plate. **Benefits** Working with us has its perks! People are at the heart of everything we do, so we've developed a range of benefits and development opportunities: * Great operational training and development * Life assurance scheme * Personal Development and Training opportunities \- We are passionate about our growing team * A great wellbeing strategy \- including access to our Employee Assistance Programme * Regular social events and communication with our leaders * A holiday purchase scheme * Access to some great high street discount vouchers Here's an idea of what your shift pattern will be: * Mon: Full\-time (Days) * Tues: Full\-time (Days) * Weds: Full\-time (Days) * Thurs: Full\-time (Days) * Fri: Full\-time (Days) * Sat: * Sun: Full\-time (Days) Could you bring your spark to Compass Group Ireland? Here's what you need to know before applying: **Your Key Responsibilities Will Include** * Assisting in keeping the lobby and other public areas clean and tidy * Helping housekeeping, restaurant and banqueting staff to set up rooms or move furniture * Handling certain reception duties, such as booking theatre tickets or making restaurant reservations * Responding to customer requests where required * Running errands * Ensuring the safety of customers by, for instance, placing and removing signage that indicates wet floors or paints **Our Ideal Porter Will** * Be adaptable and easily embrace changing priorities * Be a brilliant communicator and easily build relationships * Have experience delivering high quality customer care * Take initiative and make decisions that are right for our customers * Possess the ability to work under pressure * Demonstrate exceptional timekeeping and reliability Job Reference: com/2105/42096001/52807993/ROI \#G\&G Ireland CH\&CO, a part of Compass Group UK\&I, is committed to fostering an environment where every individual can truly be themselves at work and has equal opportunities to advance in their careers. We strive to build a culture that respects and celebrates the unique talents, beliefs, backgrounds, and abilities of all our team members. We want our colleagues to feel valued, empowered to reach their full potential, and to thrive \- because diversity is our strength!

title: Portfolio Management Compliance Analyst
description: **Overview** **The Opportunity:** The Portfolio Management Compliance (“PMC”) Analyst (“Analyst”) is responsible for helping to perform Portfolio Management Compliance team tasks, requirements, and protocols within the Fisher Group of companies. The PMC Analyst works cross\-functionally with the members of the Fisher Group Legal, Communications, and Compliance Department (“LCCD”) to help Fisher entities satisfy their portfolio management regulatory requirements. The Analyst is a member of the Portfolio Management Compliance team, who provide oversight and challenge of the Fisher Group fulfilment of trading\-related requirements. **The Day\-to\-Day:** * Monitors compliance of the Fisher trading function business to completely and efficiently meet regulatory requirements, particularly in relation to regulatory reporting * Ensure all required trade reporting to local regulators of the Fisher Group is being met in a timely and accurate manner, according to MiFIR and EMIR guidelines, elevating to a senior member of the team if issues arise * Monitor regulatory changes which affect the business and perform gap analysis on those regulations * Monitors internal trade process adherence reporting, elevating flags as needed * Works closely with the CGA team (Client Guidelines and Assurance), as appropriate * Responsible for quarterly and annual funds (US, Australian, Irish, Canadian) investment fund manager attestations and coordinating with Finance on regulatory reporting * Coordinates oversight for Commodity Pool Operators (CPOs) and Commodity Trading Advisors (CTA) exemptions and adherence * Responsible for procedure review and updates * Maintain a formal process for International Swaps and Derivatives Association (ISDA) compliance, in addition to the Options Clearing Corporation (OCC IDs), Depository Trust \& Clearing Corporation (DTCC) and other market facing portals * Contributes towards IMAs and RFPs * Support projects related to regulatory reporting * Works onsite, under direct supervision, in a team\-based and open office environment **Your Qualifications:** * Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent combination of education and experience required * 2\+ years of experience in investment management regulatory compliance preferred * Working knowledge of both US and European regulatory framework, particularly USPR, EMIR, and MiFID II preferred * Proven experience of trade execution within a high\-volume trading environment, delivering consistent performance under pressure * Superior attention to detail and excellent organisational skills * Excellent oral and written communication skills * Experience at working both independently and in a team\-oriented, collaborative environment * Ability to work independently and conform to shifting priorities, demands and timelines through analytical and problem\-solving capabilities * Working knowledge of MS Office * Able to interface with different departments within the company **Why Fisher Investments Ireland:** The global Fisher organisation distinguishes itself by putting clients first, providing unmatched service, and taking a personalised approach to investing. You can feel confident knowing that Fisher Investments and Fisher Investments Ireland align with our clients’ priorities by using a simple and transparent fee structure and recognised European custodians. It’s the people that make the Fisher purpose possible, and to help our employees meet their long\-term goals, we offer an array of benefits, including: * 100% paid premiums for our top\-tier supplemental healthcare and dental plans for employees and their qualified dependents * 28 days annual leave, with the ability to purchase up to 3 additional days per year, plus up to 11 paid holidays * Enhanced maternity pay package with 26 weeks’ top up to full base pay for eligible employees * $10,000\* fertility, hormonal health and family\-forming benefit * A retirement pension plan, featuring a 9% company pension contribution of your base pay and an additional company match of up to 5% of base pay * Monthly gym subsidy of up to €50 per month * Employee Assistance Program and other emotional wellbeing services * A collaborative working environment that practices ongoing training, educational support and employee appreciation events * This is an in\-office role. Based on your role, tenure, and performance eligibility you may have the opportunity to participate in our hybrid work from home program. This program is subject to change. * Employees residing outside of the US will be eligible for the $10,000 equivalent in their local currency. FISHER INVESTMENTS IRELAND IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

title: Process Operator, Rendering, Nobber, Meath
description: College Group was founded in 1989 and currently operates from three sites; in Meath, Cavan and Down. This position will be an integral part of our operation and will work to maximize efficiency and support delivery of key performance indicators. The role will operate on an 8 hour shift pattern rostered over days and nights which will include weekends. **ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES** * Operate the production PLC system to control production, gather data and generate reports * Identify and report any problems to the production supervisor * Achieve process capacity and KPIs * Hand\-over of key information at shift transitions * Sample and deliver products to the lab for QC * Perform general facility maintenance and house\-keeping including equipment inspection and preventative maintenance * Follow all safety guidelines and rules on\-site regulated by the HSA and other regulatory authorities **EXPERIENCE REQUIRED** * Ability to organize and prioritize work for timely completion * Good computer skills * Strong attention to detail * Effective communication skills Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * On\-site parking * Private medical insurance * Sick pay * Wellness program Application question(s): * Have you 2\+ years' experience welding / fabricating?
